Agentic / Semantic Layer Importance: Model-level NL instructions that customize the Gemini agent's identity, vocabulary, and query behavior without changing the LookML schema — glossary terms extend the NL vocabulary with official definitions; additional_descriptions encode business rules like default date ranges and column preference.
Human Importance: Low — invisible to users; experienced as a more contextually aware and accurate Conversational Analytics agent
Agentic Importance: High — system instructions are the highest-ROI AI context investment for Looker; a well-authored instruction document with glossary terms can dramatically improve NL query accuracy on domain-specific questions
Snowflake Notes / Pros & Cons: Pro: Snowflake has two separate AI instruction clauses in the DDL for SQL generation and question categorization. Con: Less structured than Looker's glossary format.
Databricks Notes / Pros & Cons: N/A
dbt MetricFlow Equivalent: Not applicable
Other Platform Notes: Power BI: AI Instructions in LSDL (most similar in structure — freeform NL text); ThoughtSpot: Data Model Instructions; AtScale SML: no equivalent

Description: Setting meta: ai: searchable: true on a string dimension causes the Cube AI API to vector-index that dimension's data values — enabling semantic fuzzy matching of natural language filter values to actual database values (e.g. 'Apple phones' → 'iPhone 15 Pro Max').
Agentic / Semantic Layer Importance: Prevents the most common literal filter failure in agent queries — users say 'Apple products' but the filter value must be exactly 'Apple Inc.'; value-level semantic search eliminates this class of query failure without manual synonym enumeration.
Human Importance: Low — invisible to users; experienced as more accurate filter suggestions
Agentic Importance: High — value-level semantic search is one of the most effective mechanisms for improving agent filter accuracy on high-cardinality string dimensions; critical for product, customer, and category filtering
Snowflake Notes / Pros & Cons: Pro: Snowflake cortex_search_service links a dimension to an entire Cortex Search index — more powerful than a single searchable flag. Pro: Fuzzy matching at the Snowflake engine level.
Databricks Notes / Pros & Cons: Con: UC MV has no value-level semantic search integration; filter values must be exact matches.
dbt MetricFlow Equivalent: Not applicable — dbt relies on exact filter values
Other Platform Notes: Snowflake: cortex_search_service (most similar and more powerful); ThoughtSpot: index_type and ThoughtSpot's search engine provide value-level search

Property: MCP (Model Context Protocol) — cross-platform transport standard
Description: JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol (Anthropic, Nov 2024; donated to Linux Foundation Dec 2025; adopted by OpenAI and Google DeepMind) defining how AI agents discover and invoke tools from any service; all major semantic layer vendors now have or are building MCP servers that expose their semantic layer capabilities as structured tools.
Agentic / Semantic Layer Importance: MCP is the emerging de facto standard for how semantic layers expose themselves to AI agents — not a content format but a transport/API convention; a semantic model with an MCP server is immediately accessible to any MCP-compatible agent (Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, etc.) without custom integration work.
Human Importance: N/A — infrastructure transport protocol; not a semantic model property
Agentic Importance: High — MCP servers are the primary mechanism through which AI agents will query semantic layers in 2025–2026; every platform's MCP server is the most important integration surface for agentic use
Snowflake Notes / Pros & Cons: Pro: Snowflake Cortex Analyst exposed via Snowflake Intelligence as an MCP tool. Con: Proprietary endpoint not an open MCP server package.
Databricks UC Equivalent: Databricks Mosaic AI Tool (Genie exposed via Unity Catalog MCP)
Databricks Notes / Pros & Cons: Pro: Genie accessible via Databricks Agent Bricks and MCP marketplace. Con: Databricks-specific ecosystem.
dbt MetricFlow Equivalent: dbt MCP server (remote — GA at Coalesce 2025; local via uvx dbt-mcp)
Cube.dev Equivalent: Cube AI API (REST / GraphQL / SQL wire protocol — MCP integration available)
Other Platform Notes: ThoughtSpot: MCP server (announced Mar 2026); Looker: MCP Toolbox (Jul 2025); AtScale: MCP server (public preview); Power BI: Modeling MCP server (Nov 2025 preview)
